figures.caption: Figure 1: Given a dataset and optional context, EFE uses metadata, statistics, and past feedback to prompt an LLM to propose fit/transform programs. Candidates are evaluated against the identity baseline, and scores are fed back into an evolutionary loop.

Figure 2: (a) Effect of the evolved transform on forecasting: Top: Chronos-2 on a steep exponential series under-extrapolates vs. ground truth; Middle: after the transform the series is near-stationary and forecasts better match the transformed truth; Bottom: inverse-transforming the forecast yields a much closer fit. (b) Aggregate forecast metrics on a synthetic exponential-growth benchmark (100 series, length 5000, 128-step horizon): NO FE uses raw inputs, EFE-Time forecasts in transformed space and inverts back.

Figure 3: Cross-model transfer of EFE-Time. The programs were evolved against Chronos-2 only and reused without modification on TimesFM-2.5, Moirai-2-Small, and Reverso-Nano. (a) Average percent improvement across Covid Deaths, M4-yearly, and solar hourly. (b) Same setup averaged across all 10 GIFT-Eval datasets in Table 1. We report average scores across three replicates.
Table 2: Comparison of feature engineering methods for single decision trees across tabular datasets over ROC-AUC. The reported results are 3 outer-fold averages from the TabArena benchmark.

Figure 6: (a) Single decision tree over 3 folds × 5 seeds (15 runs per cell). (b) Test AUC vs. training-set size for TabPFN. EFE-Tab features deliver a larger lift in the low-data regime, with the gap shrinking as more training data becomes available. (c) Average ∆ AUC (EFE-Tab - baseline) over the held-out test and validation sets vs. the fraction of training data exposed to the evolution loop, averaged over TabPFN/LightGBM/decision-tree and 3 seeds on churn and in_vehicle_coupon_recommendation.
Table 4: The TabArena datasets used in the EFE-Tab evaluation.

Figure 7: Per-checkpoint validation and test MASE ratios for the covid_deaths and solar_H model-size scans. Faint traces are individual seeds; solid lines are per-size means; ×-marks denote the iteration at which each run’s optimizer-final program was first found. Values below the dashed line (ratio=1) improve over the Chronos-2 identity-transform baseline.
Table 7: Performance comparison of feature engineering methods across tabular datasets over ROCAUC. The reported results are 3 outer-fold averages from the TabArena benchmark.
Figure 8: The change of validation and test MASE during EFE-Time evolution for Covid-Deaths.
Figure 9: The change of validation and test MASE during EFE-Time evolution for Solar-hourly.
Figure 15: Evolution trees for the three covid_deaths runs (seeds 1, 2, 3 from left to right). All three seeds locate programs that cross the 10%-improvement line.
Figure 16: Evolution trees for the three m4_yearly runs. The seed-to-seed score variability is visibly wider than on covid_deaths.
Figure 17: Evolution trees for the three solar_H runs. The three panels are visually similar.
Figure 18: Evolution trees for the three restaurant runs. The optimizer-selected scores sit only marginally above the identity baseline.
Figure 19: Evolution trees for the three bitbrains_rnd/H runs. As on restaurant, the populations crowd a vertical band around the identity baseline.
